semanticCore: Shedding blood, pouring out fluid, flow, speech speed, or dump

keyDerivations.meaning: To shed, pour out, spill, cause to flow (specifically blood)

figurativeOrRareUsages: أَتَجَعَلُ فِيهَا مَنْ يُفْسِدُ فِيهَا وَيَسْفِكُ الدِّمَاءَ (2:30): (Angels asking God): 'Will You place upon it one who causes corruption therein and sheds blood?'
summaryEn: The root represents spilling and shedding blood (Safk), capturing the angels' predictive concern about human violence and ordering Israel to ban mutual blood-shedding.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر إراقة الدماء وصب السوائل بكثرة (سفك)، واصفاً مخاوف الملائكة من عنف البشر المفسد (ويسفك الدماء)، وميثاق منع القتل.
rawText: اسفك 7 , said of blood, (K, TA,) and tears, and water, and any fluid or liquid, (TA,) It was, or became, shed, poured forth, or caused to run or flow; or it poured forth, or flowed. (K, TA.) تسافكوا دِمَآءَهُمْ 6 [They contended together in the mutual shedding of blood]. (TA in art. نجز) سفّكهُ 2 ,inf. n. تَسْفِيكٌ, He fed him (i. e. his guest) with something whereby to content, or satisfy, him, [so as to allay the craving of his stomach,] before the morning-meal; i. q. لَمَّجَهُ (O, TA.) سَافِكٌ Shedding, or pouring forth, blood and tears [&c.]. (Msb.) You say عُيُونٌ سَوَافِكُ Eyes shedding, or pouring forth, tears. (TA.) === And دُمُوعٌ سَوَافِكُ [Tears pouring forth; properly] meaning ذَوَاتُ سَفْكٍ [having a shedding or pouring forth], the latter word being pl. of سَافِكَةٌ: so as used in a verse of Mutemmim Ibn-Nuweyreh: but the obviously-right expression is 🡻مَسْفُوكَةٌ (Ham p. 370.) سَفَكَ 1 , aor. ﹻ (S, M, O, Msb, K) and ﹹ, (O, Msb, TA, &c.,) inf. n. سَفْكٌ, (S, O, Msb,) He shed, poured forth, or caused to run or flow, blood, (S, O, Msb, K, TA,) and tears, (S, O, Msb, TA,) and water, and any fluid or liquid, but app. most especially blood. (TA.) === And [hence,] سَفَكَ الكَلَامَ, (K,) inf. n. as above, (TA,) ‡ He poured forth speech profusely, (K, TA,) with haste, or quickly. (TA.) سَفُوكٌ : see سَفَّاكٌ, in two places. === Also † The soul: (K:) [like كَذُوبٌ: app. because of its proneness to lying.] سَفِيكٌ Blood, (K, TA,) and tears, and water, and any fluid or liquid, (TA,) Shed, poured forth, or caused to run or flow; as also 🡻مَسْفُوكٌ (K, TA.) سَفَّاكٌ One who sheds blood, and tears, much; (Msb;) [as also 🡻سَفُوكٌ.] You say سَفَّاكٌ لِلدِّمَآءِ A great shedder of blood. (TA.) === And † Eloquent; (Kr, K;) an able speaker. (S, K.) === And ‡ Mendacious; a great, or habitual, liar; (TA;) as also 🡻سَفُوكٌ, (K,) or سَفُوكٌ بَالكَلَامِ (TA.) سُفْكَةٌ A portion of food that is presented to a guest, to content, or satisfy, him, [so as to allay the craving of his stomach,] before the morningmeal; like لُمْجَةٌ. (IAar, O, K. *) مِسْفَكٌ † Loquacious; garrulous. (K, * TA.) مِسْفُوكٌ : See سَفِيكٌ and سَافِكٌ.
